original_hypothesis.title: Banning Vault Address Causes Permanent Loss of In-Flight Bridged Tokens
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When a vault address is added to addressBanned, processMessage handles messages targeting the vault by setting refundAmount=_message.value, marking status as DONE, and refunding ETH. However, the token transfer data encoded in _message.data is never executed (no onMessageInvocation call). On the source chain, the tokens were already locked/burned. Since the message status is DONE (not FAILED), recallMessage cannot be invoked. The tokens are permanently lost.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The banned address handling in processMessage marks messages as DONE without executing the payload. For vault-targeted messages carrying token operations, this creates an irrecoverable token loss since the recall path requires FAILED status.

proof_of_signal.pattern: addressBanned[_message.to] → refundAmount = _message.value, status = DONE; token data in _message.data never processed

preconditions: bridge_watchdog bans a vault address (maliciously or accidentally)
Messages are in-flight targeting the banned vault
Messages contain token transfer operations in their data

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Banned target → mark DONE with ETH refund; token data ignored; recall impossible since status is DONE not FAILED
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: DONE status prevents recall on source chain; tokens locked/burned on source with no recovery
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No mechanism to mark banned messages as FAILED instead of DONE

analysis_questions: How many in-flight messages could be affected by a vault ban?
Could the ban be reversed and messages re-processed?
Is there any upgrade path to recover the lost tokens?
validation_requirements: Ban vault address, process in-flight message, verify DONE status
Attempt recall on source chain, confirm failure
Verify tokens remain locked in source vault

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Watchdog accidentally or maliciously bans vault address. All pending messages to that vault are processed as DONE with only ETH refunded. Token operations in message data are skipped. Source chain tokens are permanently locked since recall requires FAILED status.
prerequisites: In-flight messages exist targeting the vault

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Permanent loss of all in-flight token transfers to the banned vault
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users with pending bridge transfers to the affected vault
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: None through normal protocol operation; requires upgrade

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for banning vault address with in-flight token messages

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Observable: DONE messages with token data that were never executed

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
